faqs.question: How much does carpenter ant control cost in Fort Lee, NJ?
faqs.answer: Carpenter ant control in Fort Lee typically costs between $350 and $2,000, depending on the size of your property, severity of the infestation, and number of colonies present. faqs.question: How can I tell if I have carpenter ants versus regular ants in Fort Lee?
faqs.answer: Carpenter ants are significantly larger than common household ants (typically ½ inch long), black or dark reddish-brown in color, and have a single-segmented waist. The most telling signs are piles of wood shavings (frass) that look like sawdust near wooden structures, faint rustling sounds in walls, and winged reproductive ants emerging in spring. Unlike termites, carpenter ants don't eat wood but excavate it to build nests.

faqs.question: Why are carpenter ants so common in Fort Lee?
faqs.answer: Fort Lee's location along the wooded Palisades, humid climate near the Hudson River, and abundance of mature trees create perfect conditions for carpenter ants. Many of the borough's older buildings have wood-frame construction with moisture issues from aging plumbing or poor ventilation, which attracts carpenter ants seeking damp, decaying wood for nesting. The area's dense development also helps colonies spread easily between structures.

faqs.question: How long does carpenter ant treatment take to work in Fort Lee homes?
faqs.answer: Most Fort Lee residents see significant reduction in carpenter ant activity within 7-10 days after treatment, with complete elimination typically achieved within 2-4 weeks. Treatment effectiveness depends on locating and treating all satellite colonies, which our technicians accomplish through thorough inspection. We schedule follow-up visits at 2-3 weeks post-treatment to ensure the colony has been fully eliminated and provide additional treatment if needed at no extra charge.
